jokes.text: And then thanks for the birthday card that I never got from you for the last 40 years.
jokes.setup: Father tells Gob to work for his younger brother
jokes.punchline: Gob sarcastically thanks for 40 years of non-existent birthday cards
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Gob's passive-aggressive complaint reveals family dysfunction while being petty and specific

jokes.text: ...that led to this tinkling match.
jokes.punchline: Narrator calls their one-upping a 'tinkling match' instead of 'pissing contest'
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism that maintains the narrator's mock-sophisticated tone while being appropriately childish

jokes.text: I've met someone. Can't really give you any information-- kind of famousy.
jokes.setup: Gob claims to be dating someone
jokes.punchline: Makes up the word 'famousy' to describe his mystery girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob - invents a word to sound sophisticated while being completely transparent about trying to one-up Michael

jokes.text: Also famousy. Can't give you any information.
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately copies the made-up word
jokes.explanation: Quick callback showing Michael getting sucked into Gob's childish competition and linguistic invention

jokes.text: Is it Julie Bowen? / No, it's not. / Is yours Julie Bowen?
jokes.setup: Brothers won't reveal who they're dating
jokes.punchline: They immediately start guessing the same specific celebrity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures sibling rivalry - they're so similar they guess the same random celebrity

jokes.text: I want to hear you say the words 'I'm not seeing Julie Bowen.' / I have not seen Julie Bowen. / Well, that's a funny way to phrase it.
jokes.setup: Gob demands specific denial about Julie Bowen
jokes.punchline: Michael gives technically different answer, Gob notices the evasion
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows both characters' lawyerly evasiveness while building paranoia

jokes.text: But soon the hard lemonade softened their competitive edge.
jokes.punchline: Hard lemonade 'softened' their edge - alcohol pun
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that works but isn't particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: No, they don't.
jokes.setup: Gob says houses sell themselves, then clarifies
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - confidently making a claim then immediately undercutting it

jokes.text: I'm not talking about selling these mausoleums. I mean destroying Tony Wonder.
jokes.punchline: Gob reveals his real obsession with rival magician
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Gob's priorities - revenge over business, plus 'mausoleums' is a great descriptor

jokes.text: I need you to act as my boyfriend to help me get in.
jokes.setup: Gob wants to destroy Tony Wonder at gay club
jokes.punchline: Asks Michael to pretend to be his boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the episode's central premise while being genuinely uncomfortable and funny

jokes.text: I am your brother. / You sound like my son.
jokes.punchline: Gob compares him to his son, setting up flashback
jokes.explanation: Good transition that shows Gob gets similar rejections from family members

jokes.text: I love magic, of course. I mean, my dad's a magician. Well, you're my dad, I mean, so, so you should know.
jokes.setup: Steve talking to his father Gob
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly clarifies the obvious family relationship
jokes.explanation: Shows the awkward dynamic between Gob and his son - they barely know each other

jokes.text: Well, technically gay night at a magic club.
jokes.setup: Describing the venue as a gay club
jokes.punchline: Corrects himself with ridiculous specificity
jokes.explanation: Gob's need to be technically correct about ridiculous details

jokes.text: and then thanks for the birthday card that I never got from you for the last 40 years.
jokes.punchline: Uses exact same complaint he used on his father
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback showing Gob's recycling of grievances across generations

jokes.text: This was extra hard for Steve to hear on his actual birthday.
jokes.setup: Gob complains about never getting birthday cards
jokes.punchline: Reveals Steve is having this conversation on his birthday
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - the cruel irony of complaining about birthday neglect to your son on his birthday

jokes.text: that wasn't for revenge, that was just on spec.
jokes.setup: Discussing previous attempt to lock Tony Wonder
jokes.punchline: Gob treats magic sabotage like a business proposal
jokes.explanation: Absurd application of business terminology to petty magic rivalry

jokes.text: Jesus. / No, that would be a 'J.' This is a 'T.'
jokes.punchline: Gob thinks he's identifying the letter T
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - Gob's literal interpretation of an exclamation shows his obliviousness

jokes.text: He got the last laugh, which, in this case, happened to be the first laugh.
jokes.setup: Explaining Tony Wonder got revenge first
jokes.punchline: Confusing temporal explanation about first/last laughs
jokes.explanation: Gob's attempt to sound profound while being completely confusing

jokes.text: Listen, if you insist on speaking to me like you're my wife, then don't be surprised to find my [bleep] in you and then you never hear from me again.
jokes.punchline: Gob makes inappropriate sexual threat/comparison
jokes.explanation: Pure Gob inappropriateness - making everything sexual and hostile

jokes.text: I should have my attorney Bob Loblaw look at this.
jokes.punchline: References the recurring character with the ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Standard callback to running character name gag

jokes.text: It's Gob's Arrested Development.
jokes.punchline: Show title modified for character focus
jokes.explanation: Standard but effective title modification that plays on double meaning

jokes.text: Do I seem like a clown to you? / I got it. / Do I seem like some kind of a clown to you?
jokes.setup: Gob asking if he seems like a clown
jokes.punchline: Immediately repeats with slight variation
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's insecurity and need for validation through repetition

jokes.text: No, the Gothicassle. Yes, the Gothic Castle. / No, I didn't say the Gothicassle.
jokes.punchline: Gob gets confused by his own mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay that becomes a mini-running gag within the scene

jokes.text: I think you look amazing. / I'll take it. / Great. / No, no. The compliment, not the shirt.
jokes.punchline: Gob clarifies he's taking the compliment, not buying anything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - taking validation while avoiding spending money

jokes.text: which, that night, did actually kind of resemble the Gothicassle.
jokes.punchline: Narrator uses Gob's mispronunciation while describing the visual
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that validates Gob's mispronunciation with visual evidence

jokes.text: I do know that your daddy wants you to get your hot, little, tight, little, hot ass right up there right away to see him.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual language toward family member
jokes.explanation: Pure cringe - Gob's inappropriate sexuality applied to his own son

jokes.text: and I just needed a believable arm candy bean.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he brought George Michael
jokes.punchline: Combines 'arm candy' with 'bean' (family nickname)
jokes.explanation: Clever combination of terms that's both specific to the family and situation

jokes.text: And don't make that face; you look homophobic.
jokes.punchline: Gob misinterprets discomfort as prejudice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gob's obliviousness to his nephew's actual discomfort

jokes.text: the Fakeblock thing will scrub it away. / You know about Fakeblock? / You know, that's my software.
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's revealed his secret project
jokes.explanation: Sets up major plot point while showing George Michael's pride in his fake success

jokes.text: You're giving me these craz... / Mad? / N...
jokes.punchline: Gob thinks he's saying 'mad' and interrupts
jokes.explanation: Quick wordplay moment showing miscommunication

jokes.text: Bit my tongue, that little bitch. / Oh, no, he didn't. / They're always doing that. / I mean, oh, no, he didn't.
jokes.setup: Gob complains about biting his tongue
jokes.punchline: Onlooker thinks he's talking about a person, corrects misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with the onlooker interpreting literal statement as drama

jokes.text: I never want to sleep with you again. / I mean, I probably will. I don't. / But are we good?
jokes.setup: Gob trying to maintain fake relationship cover
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself multiple times
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob - getting confused by his own cover story and being inappropriately sexual

jokes.text: Who's the dumbwaiter now?
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'dumb waiter' meaning both stupid person and service elevator
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that works on multiple levels and fits Gob's character

jokes.text: Well, I'm here, I'm queer, and now... I'm in a chair!
jokes.setup: Tony Wonder appearing from beanbag chair
jokes.punchline: Modifies famous slogan to include his current predicament
jokes.explanation: Clever modification of famous phrase that works perfectly for magic act

jokes.text: Did somebody say Wonder? / Nobody actually did that time, but I think he got away with it.
jokes.punchline: Narrator acknowledges no one said his name this time
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about the character's own running gag, showing self-awareness

jokes.text: Now everybody's gay! / They started out gay.
jokes.punchline: Narrator's matter-of-fact deflation of the 'magic'
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of magic trick by pointing out obvious context

jokes.text: Did somebody say Wonder...ing?
jokes.punchline: Stretches the word to justify his entrance
jokes.explanation: Variation on his running gag that shows his desperation to make it work

jokes.text: See, it's like a quotation mark, but with a 'W.'
jokes.punchline: Explains his signature gesture in technical terms
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tony's need to explain his signature moves

jokes.text: You should have seen me when they took me out of the storage locker.
jokes.punchline: References his humiliating Jesus trick failure
jokes.explanation: Good callback to major character failure while maintaining conversation flow

jokes.text: I even shaved them. Want to feel? / Wow, they are smooth. / Like a lady's. / I think.
jokes.setup: Tony offers to let Gob feel his shaved legs
jokes.punchline: Gob's awkward comparison and uncertainty
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment that reveals both characters' uncertainty about their fake personas

jokes.text: Valet is closing. The Cabriolet-- license plate: 'ANUS TART.'
jokes.punchline: Tobias's infamous license plate announcement
jokes.explanation: Classic callback that always gets a laugh due to the ridiculousness

jokes.text: As it turns out, they did a lot more than steal pies.
jokes.setup: Gob and Tony went out together
jokes.punchline: Narrator's suggestive but vague implication
jokes.explanation: Good narrator misdirection that implies without explicitly stating

jokes.text: Don't call me girl, friend.
jokes.setup: Tobias says Gob is into 'this girl'
jokes.punchline: Gob's defensive response with ambiguous punctuation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works both as 'girlfriend' correction and address to friend

jokes.text: Oh, I have a list of men that could fill every opening you have.
jokes.punchline: Tobias's unintentionally suggestive phrasing
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias double entendre - completely innocent intent with obvious sexual interpretation

jokes.text: That was all cheese, but no mustard.
jokes.punchline: Complains about sandwich construction
jokes.explanation: Minor visual gag that shows Gob's pickiness about mundane things

jokes.text: I'm not here right now, because I'm right behind you. Made you look. But how did I know you looked? Because I'm right behind you! Made you look again.
jokes.punchline: Overly complex magic-themed answering machine message
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Tony's commitment to magic persona even in voicemail

jokes.text: Who wants to help me build a wall to keep Mexicans out of America? [bleep] / All right, all right! Who wants to help me build a wall for no reason? It's a different wall.
jokes.punchline: Changes his pitch after negative reaction
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's complete tone-deafness and willingness to change his story when convenient

jokes.text: You should get the Chinese to build your wall. We make great walls. / I'm Chinese; I know all Chinese.
jokes.setup: Worker suggests Chinese people for wall building
jokes.punchline: Claims to know all Chinese people
jokes.explanation: Plays on stereotypes and Great Wall reference, somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Would you get off my [bleep], Mother! / Well, if I wasn't sure you were gay, I am now.
jokes.setup: Gob yells at his mother on phone
jokes.punchline: Tony interprets outburst as confirmation of gayness
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection where family dynamics are misinterpreted as sexual orientation evidence

jokes.text: Trust me, I am all man. Give me a little leg shave?
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for help shaving legs
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction showing the absurdity of his fake gay persona requiring 'unmanly' grooming

jokes.text: Poor guy doesn't realize I'm about to destroy our life. Our life? I mean, destroy our life with Gob. Dest... I'm going to destroy our life-- my life-- with Gob.
jokes.punchline: Gets increasingly confused about whose life he's destroying
jokes.explanation: Shows Tony's confusion about his own feelings and motivations

jokes.text: ♪ Hello darkness, my old friend... ♪ Destroy my life with Gob.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic Simon & Garfunkel reference while obsessing over Gob
jokes.explanation: Good use of iconic song to underscore character's melodramatic confusion

jokes.text: It seemed Sally had two secrets.
jokes.setup: Sally appearing to have moth/butterfly phobias
jokes.punchline: Narrator's ominous suggestion about additional secrets
jokes.explanation: Good setup for future revelations with classic AD mysterious narrator tone

jokes.text: I'm going to need the gayest. / Did you say the 'grayest'? / I need the, uh, gayest one. / Did you say the 'grayest'?
jokes.punchline: Siri repeatedly mishears 'gayest' as 'grayest'
jokes.explanation: Technology humor that plays on speech recognition failures

jokes.text: She's got a kid, he's acting like he's Father of the Year or something. I bet he's pretending Steve Holt is, like, nine. I just saw him, he looks 50.
jokes.setup: Someone complaining about a father figure
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Steve Holt looks 50 when pretending to be a child
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Steve Holt's actual age versus how he's treated

jokes.text: First of all, that's very dated of you, Michael. 'He's' don't like to be called 'she's'
jokes.punchline: Gob's completely wrong understanding of gender pronouns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob - trying to sound progressive while being completely confused

jokes.text: It said 'Love each other' on the foot band. / Yeah, that does sound like one of mine.
jokes.punchline: Gob casually admits to the cheesy message
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's lack of shame about his corny romantic gestures

jokes.text: It was a merry mix-up.
jokes.punchline: Narrator's cheerful summary of dark situation
jokes.explanation: Classic narrator understatement with alliteration making tragedy sound pleasant

jokes.text: It was a merry mix-up.
jokes.punchline: Narrator's cheerful understatement
jokes.explanation: Perfect AD narrator understatement for complex dramatic irony

jokes.text: This gay bar would be the best...! / ...something that can fillet a chicken and subdue an intruder.
jokes.setup: Gob starts to praise gay bar for fighting
jokes.punchline: Cut to knife commercial with violent language
jokes.explanation: Great editing joke that creates violent context from innocent setup

jokes.text: With sex offenders.
jokes.setup: Michael asks who filled the houses
jokes.punchline: Gob's matter-of-fact revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline - simple, shocking, and perfectly in character for Gob's obliviousness

jokes.text: It's, like, God... you get me. Well, you're an easy guy to get.
jokes.punchline: Tony's backhanded compliment about Gob's simplicity
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction that works on two levels - Gob misses the insult in the 'compliment'.

jokes.text: Same! I was gonna say it. Same! I was thinking the same. That's so weird! ...same. ...same. Crazy! ...zy!
jokes.punchline: Escalating 'same' game that becomes meaningless repetition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that shows how fake connection devolves into meaningless agreement. Very quotable.

jokes.text: It almost went down my throat.
jokes.setup: Popcorn fight between the two men
jokes.punchline: Gob's unintentionally suggestive complaint
jokes.explanation: Mild double entendre that's more coincidental than clever

jokes.text: I would definitely stay and have gay sex with... or normal sex with you, but...
jokes.setup: Gob trying to maintain cover story
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals he thinks they'd have 'normal' sex
jokes.explanation: Great slip showing Gob's true thoughts about their relationship

jokes.text: I have... feelings for you.
jokes.punchline: First time Gob admits to having feelings for anyone
jokes.explanation: Genuinely moving moment for a character who's never shown real emotion - both funny and touching

jokes.text: The feeling was friendship, but neither had ever experienced it.
jokes.setup: Romantic tension between Gob and Tony
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals they're just experiencing friendship for the first time
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insight that reframes the entire relationship - these shallow men have never had a real friend

jokes.text: Oui. / What? We what? / No, oui. It's French for 'yes.'
jokes.punchline: Gob completely misunderstands and thinks she's saying 'we'
jokes.explanation: Classic misunderstanding based on language confusion

jokes.text: We're going to destroy Tony's career by proving that he's straight when he has sex with you while you're wearing... this Gob mask.
jokes.punchline: Convoluted scheme involving identity confusion and sex
jokes.explanation: Peak Gob logic - overcomplicated plan that makes no sense but he's confident about it.

jokes.text: It's a straight bait.
jokes.punchline: Gob invents term playing on 'gay bait'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures his scheme

jokes.text: You think the Shubert Organization would entrust a project like Dreamgirls to a straight producer?
jokes.setup: Tony explaining fake gay business strategy
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous theory about Broadway requiring gay producers
jokes.explanation: Absurd business theory that shows Tony's elaborate justifications

jokes.text: Biggest fake gay there is, by the way.
jokes.punchline: Tony claims NPH is also fake gay
jokes.explanation: Funny because NPH is actually gay, showing Tony's paranoid worldview

jokes.text: Now everybody's gay!
jokes.punchline: Narrator uses Tony's catchphrase for the ironic situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that ironically applies to the actual situation

jokes.text: Stupid, forgetful Michael.
jokes.punchline: Calls Michael forgetful after drugging him
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Gob calls Michael forgetful after causing the forgetfulness

jokes.text: Who was that? / No one.
jokes.punchline: Rebel denies the call to someone else
jokes.explanation: Standard ending irony showing Michael still doesn't have what he wants
